testcase.name: 3220-dimension-relationship-node-linkrole-testcase
Dimension Relationship Node Linkrole Testcase
testcase.description: Table linkbase test case using a dimension relationship node with different linkroles

variation.name: Dimension relationship node with link role omitted
variation.description: Table with two columns (primary items m1, m2) and a tree of members for explicit dimension F as rows. No link role is specified, so the standard role (http://www.xbrl.org/2003/role/link) is assumed. This variation has no instance.

variation.name: Dimension relationship node with link role omitted, with instance
variation.description: Table with two columns (primary items m1, m2) and a tree of members for explicit dimension F as rows. No link role is specified, so the standard role (http://www.xbrl.org/2003/role/link) is assumed.

variation.name: Dimension relationship node with target role switch
variation.description: Table with two columns (primary items m1, m2) and a tree of members for explicit dimension F as rows. Discovering the domain-member tree involves following a target role switch. Tests that processors correctly follow consecutive relationships. This variation has no instance.

variation.name: Dimension relationship node with target role switch, with instance
variation.description: Table with two columns (primary items m1, m2) and a tree of members for explicit dimension F as rows. Discovering the domain-member tree involves following a target role switch. Tests that processors correctly follow consecutive relationships.

variation.name: Dimension relationship node with parameterized link role
variation.description: Two tables each with two columns (primary items m1, m2) and a tree of members for explicit dimension F as rows. Each table displays the domain-member tree for a different link role. This variation has no instance.

variation.name: Dimension relationship node with a link role expression that evaluates to an invalid URI
variation.description: Invalid table with a dimension relationship node that specifies the link role using an XPath expression that evaluates to an invalid URI.

variation.name: Dimension relationship node with an empty link role expression
variation.description: Invalid table with a dimension relationship node that specifies the link role using an XPath expression that evaluates to the empty string.

variation.name: Dimension relationship node with a link role expression that evaluates to a sequence
variation.description: Invalid table with a dimension relationship node that specifies the link role using an XPath expression that evaluates to a sequence of more than one URI.

variation.name: Dimension relationship node with a simple link role expression
variation.description: Simple table with two columns (periods 2001, 2002) and a tree of members for explicit dimension F as rows. The link role is specified using an XPath expression.

variation.name: Failing test case for bug 845
variation.description: The specification says: "If no relationship source is specified, the root members of the domain of the participating dimension are used as the relationship sources. Specifically, the relationship sources are the targets of dimension-domain relationships with the specified link role whose source is the participating dimension." To our understanding this means that we get all the targets of dimension-domain relationships in the give linkrole and from those starting points follow the consecutive domain-member relationships. What should happen if there are no dimension-domain relationships in the specified linkrole? Is it valid to report the xbrlte:closedDefinitionNodeZeroCardinality?

variation.name: Test case for bug 845
variation.description: The specification says: "If no relationship source is specified, the root members of the domain of the participating dimension are used as the relationship sources. Specifically, the relationship sources are the targets of dimension-domain relationships with the specified link role whose source is the participating dimension." To our understanding this means that we get all the targets of dimension-domain relationships in the give linkrole and from those starting points follow the consecutive domain-member relationships. What should happen if there are no dimension-domain relationships in the specified linkrole? Is it valid to report the xbrlte:closedDefinitionNodeZeroCardinality?

variation.name: Failing test case for bug 845 (dim-dom w/targetRole)
variation.description: The specification says: "If no relationship source is specified, the root members of the domain of the participating dimension are used as the relationship sources. Specifically, the relationship sources are the targets of dimension-domain relationships with the specified link role whose source is the participating dimension." To our understanding this means that we get all the targets of dimension-domain relationships in the give linkrole and from those starting points follow the consecutive domain-member relationships. What should happen if there are no dimension-domain relationships in the specified linkrole? Is it valid to report the xbrlte:closedDefinitionNodeZeroCardinality?

variation.name: Test case for bug 845 (dim-dom w/targetRole)
variation.description: The specification says: "If no relationship source is specified, the root members of the domain of the participating dimension are used as the relationship sources. Specifically, the relationship sources are the targets of dimension-domain relationships with the specified link role whose source is the participating dimension." To our understanding this means that we get all the targets of dimension-domain relationships in the give linkrole and from those starting points follow the consecutive domain-member relationships. What should happen if there are no dimension-domain relationships in the specified linkrole? Is it valid to report the xbrlte:closedDefinitionNodeZeroCardinality?
